sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER.
Questions 11-14: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD for each answer. Questions 15-20: Label the map below. Write the correct letter A-I, next to questions 15-20.

options: A
B
C
D
E
F
G
H
I

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C.

questions.content: The students in Akira Miyake’s study were all majoring in
options: A physics
B psychology or physics
C science, technology, engineering or mathematics

questions.content: The aim of Miyake’s study was to investigate
options: A what kind of women choose to study physics
B a way of improving women’s performance in physics
C whether fewer women than men study physics at college

questions.content: The female physics students were wrong to believe that
options: A the teachers marked them in an unfair way
B the male students expected them to do badly
C their test results were lower than the male students’

questions.content: Miyake’s team asked the students to write about
options: A what they enjoyed about studying physics
B the successful experiences of other people
C something that was important to them personally

questions.content: What was the aim of the writing exercise done by the subjects?
options: A to reduce stress
B to strengthen verbal ability
C to encourage logical thinking

questions.content: What surprised the researchers about the study?
options: A how few students managed to get A grades
B the positive impact it had on physics results for women
C the difference between male and female performance

questions.content: Greg and Lisa think Miyake’s results could have been affected by
options: A the length of the writing task
B the number of students who took part
C the information the students were given

questions.content: Greg and Lisa decide that in their own project, they will compare the effects of
options: A two different writing tasks
B a writing task with an oral task
C two different oral tasks

questions.content: The main finding of Smolinsky’s research was that class teamwork activities
options: A were most effective when done by all-women groups
B had no effect on the performance of men or women
C improved the results of men more than of women

questions.content: What will Lisa and Greg do next?
options: A talk to a professor
B observe a science class
C look at the science timetable

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.

questions.content:
Biodiversity hotspots
• areas containing many different species
• important for locating targets for (31) {{BLANK}}
• at first only identified on land

Boris Worm, 2005
• identified hotspots for large ocean predators, e.g. sharks
• found that ocean hotspots:
– were not always rich in (32) {{BLANK}}
– had higher temperatures at the (33) {{BLANK}}
– had sufficient (34) {{BLANK}} in the water

Lisa Ballance, 2007
• looked for hotspots for marine (35) {{BLANK}}
• found these were all located where ocean currents meet

Census of Marine Life
• found new ocean species living:
– under the (36) {{BLANK}}
– near volcanoes on the ocean floor

Global Marine Species Assessment
• want to list endangered ocean species, considering:
– population size
– geographical distribution
– rate of (37) {{BLANK}}
• Aim: to assess 20,000 species and make a distribution (38) {{BLANK}} for each one

Recommendations to retain ocean biodiversity
• increase the number of ocean reserves
• establish (39) {{BLANK}} corridors (e.g. for turtles)
• reduce fishing quotas
• catch fish only for the purpose of (40) {{BLANK}}
